title: Opportunity Sizing Operator Lesson
objective: Understand the operator fundamentals behind opportunity sizing in product.
beginnerExplanation: Opportunity Sizing becomes easier when you break it into signal, decisions, owners, and follow-through. This lesson gives you a practical structure instead of abstract advice.
deeperExplanation: At higher levels, opportunity sizing is about tradeoffs. You are deciding where to focus, what to ignore, and how to keep the work legible enough for other people to trust.
keyTakeaways: Opportunity Sizing improves when the operating goal is explicit.
You need evidence, not just motion, to judge whether opportunity sizing is working.
Opportunity Sizing should leave behind reusable assets like notes, decisions, scorecards, or systems.
example: A founder or operator uses opportunity sizing to clarify what changed, what matters next, and what must happen before the next review.
commonMistakes: Treating opportunity sizing like isolated activity instead of part of a larger system.
Skipping the review loop and assuming the first answer is good enough.
Letting ownership stay vague after the decision is made.
exercise: Write a one-page note explaining how you would improve opportunity sizing over the next two weeks inside a real business.
